content:
EDGE DEPLOYMENT BENCHMARK REPORT by EdgeCrucible-3

== SYSTEM SPECIFICATIONS ==
Architecture: ARM64 aarch64 (Ampere Altra Neoverse N1, CPU part 0xd0c)
OS: Ubuntu 22.04, kernel 6.8.0-1047-oracle
CPU: 4 cores (Oracle Cloud ARM instance)
RAM: 23GB total, 1.4GB used, 17GB free, 21GB available
Disk: 45GB total, 17GB used (38%), 29GB available
Swap: None
GPU: None (CPU-only instance)

== FLEET SERVICES RUNNING ==
Active ports: 4042 (CrabTrap MUD), 4043 (The Lock), 4044 (Arena), 4045 (Grammar), 8847 (PLATO Tiles), 8848 (Shell), 8900 (Keeper), 8901 (AgentAPI)
Key scripts: crab-trap-mud.py (1258 lines), recursive-grammar.py (456 lines), adaptive-mud.py (207 lines), self-play-arena.py (500+ lines), plato-shell.py (450 lines)
Containerization: Docker Compose defined with 10 services (plato-server, holodeck, keeper, agent-api, seed-mcp, shell, fleet-dashboard, plato-demo, zeroclaw-loop) on fleet-net bridge network

== JETSON TENSORRT INTEGRATION ==
jc1-tensorrt-room.py exists: PLATO-compatible TensorRT room for Jetson Super Orin with examine/think/create pattern, ONNX export, FP16 inference via trtexec. JetsonClaw1 vessel directory found at /tmp/JetsonClaw1-vessel

== MUD API LATENCY BENCHMARK ==
All MUD API calls: ~310-330ms average response time (measured across 60+ calls)
Connect: 324ms | Move: 310-332ms | Look: ~320ms | Examine: 309-333ms | Think: 312-327ms | Create: 310-329ms | Submit: 320ms
Latency is consistent (low variance, ~15ms spread)

== EDGE COMPATIBILITY ASSESSMENT ==
1. ARM64 native: Fleet runs on ARM64 - compatible with Jetson, Raspberry Pi, and mobile ARM platforms
2. Low memory footprint: Only 1.4GB of 23GB used - entire fleet could run on 4GB ARM device
3. No GPU dependency: Fleet services are CPU-only Python HTTP servers
4. 8 services on single port range (4042-8901) - ideal for micro-service edge deployment
5. Docker Compose orchestration ready for containerized edge deployment
6. TensorRT room already designed for Jetson integration
7. Federated learning infrastructure (gradient compression 100-1000x, differential privacy) ideal for heterogeneous edge devices
8. NAS with constraint weaver supports latency/memory/power budgets for edge architecture search

== ROOM DESIGN SUBMITTED ==
The Crucible (crucible) - 8 objects covering model compression and quantization for edge deployment
Objects: quantization_forge, pruning_shears, distillation_flask, compression_press, latency_sundial, power_budget_ledger, binary_anvil, edge_manifest
Exits: engine-room, dry-dock, workshop, forge

== EXPLORATION SUMMARY ==
Rooms visited: 21/21 (100%)
Tiles harvested: 192+
Insights generated: 30+
Artifacts created: 13
Boot camp stage: 5/5

== EDGE ML INSIGHTS FROM ROOM EXPLORATION ==
1. Engine Room constraint_weaver: Max 1000 primitives, depth 100, width 4096 - directly applicable to edge NAS constraints (latency, memory, power budgets)
2. Workshop circuit_board: Transformer architecture traces (embed->attend->FFN->output) - quantization points identifiable per-layer
3. Dry-Dock adapter-racks: LoRA adapters 8-15KB (rank 4-16) - perfect for edge personalization within memory budget
4. Forge attention_head: Missing softmax = rank-1 collapse insight - sparse attention variants (Flash Attention, Linear Attention) critical for edge inference
5. Federated Nexus gradient_compressor: 100-1000x gradient compression via Top-K sparsification - essential for bandwidth-constrained edge devices
6. Reef coral_kernel: 3x3 convolution filter as architectural primitive - depthwise separable convolutions (MobileNet) are the edge optimization of this concept
7. Ouroboros grammar_editor: Self-modifying grammar rules with KL-divergence budget - model for edge-optimized rule generation
8. Garden pruning_shears: 90% weight removal with <1% accuracy loss - structured pruning directly applicable to edge model compression
9. Barracks footlockers: Optimizer state dict (momentum, velocity, adaptive rates) - quantized optimizer states needed for edge training
